instructions: 1. Copy this file to your project root as 'config.json'
2. Customize the structure to match your project needs
3. Run 'olli init' to scaffold the directory structure
IMPORTANT: 'olli init' requires either:
- No existing .olli/ directory, OR
- An empty .olli/ directory containing only config.json
If .olli/ already contains capsules or other files,
'olli init' will refuse to run to prevent data loss.
Use 'olli init --force' to scaffold missing directories
without touching existing files.
CUSTOMIZATION GUIDE:
categories: Top-level organizational folders.
- Default: 'application' (what you build) and 'development' (how you build)
- Rename or add categories to match your workflow
- Example: a research team might use 'analysis' and 'methodology'
roles: Subdirectories within each category.
- Default: spec, strategy, discovery, history, research
- Each role also gets a 'drafts' subdirectory automatically
- Add roles like 'testing', 'integration', or 'architecture'
- Remove roles you don't need
shared: Directories at the environment root, outside any category.
- 'extractions' is where stream extraction artifacts are stored
- Add shared directories for cross-cutting concerns
scaffold_empty: When true, creates all directories on init
even if they have no capsules yet. Recommended for visibility.
To use the default structure, delete this file and run 'olli init'.
Olliver will scaffold the standard layout automatically.

application.description: What you are building — product specs, strategy, and research

development.description: How you build it — workflow, tooling, and build discipline
